parsed_generation.narration: A person wearing a hennin headdress steps into a vaulted 15th-century court. In the first shot, the figure enters the space at eye level, the wide frame establishing the grandeur of the hall and anchoring the topic in a specific era. In the second shot, the hennin-wearing figure moves along a marble aisle as heraldic banners rise behind, the medium framing keeping the subject in balance with the courtly backdrop. In the third shot, the figure pauses beside a throne as torchlight catches the headdress, a low-angle close-up heightening the presence of status and ritual. Taken together, the three shots follow a single subject through a historical setting, illustrating what it means to walk through a 15th-century court in ceremonial dress.
